translations.text: And David took more wives at Jerusalem: and David begot more sons and daughters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the names of the children which he had in Jerusalem: Shammua, and Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Ibhar, and Elishua, and Elpelet,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Nogah, and Nepheg, and Japhia,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Elishama, and Beeliada, and Eliphelet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistines heard that David was anointed king over all Israel, and all the Philistines went up to seek David; and David heard [of it], and went out against them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistines came and spread themselves in the valley of Rephaim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David inquired ofGod saying, Shall I go up against the Philistines, and wilt thou give them into my hand? And Jehovah said to him, Go up; and I will give them into thy hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came up to Baal-perazim, and David smote them there; and David said,God has broken in upon mine enemies by my hand, as the breaking forth of waters. Therefore they called the name of that place Baal-perazim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they left theirgods there; and David commanded, and they were burned with fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Philistines yet again spread themselves in the valley.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David inquired again ofGod; andGod said to him, Go not up after them; turn round them and come upon them opposite the mulberry-trees.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be, when thou hearest the sound of marching in the tops of the mulberry-trees, that then thou shalt go out to battle; forGod will have gone forth before thee to smite the army of the Philistines.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David did asGod commanded him; and they smote the army of the Philistines from Gibeon even to Gezer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the fame of David went out into all lands; and Jehovah brought the fear of him upon all the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made him houses in the city of David, and prepared a place for the ark ofGod, and spread a tent for it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then David said, None ought to carry the ark ofGod but the Levites, for them has Jehovah chosen to carry the ark ofGod, and to serve him for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David assembled all Israel to Jerusalem, to bring up the ark of Jehovah to its place that he had prepared for it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David gathered the sons of Aaron and the Levites:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of the sons of Kohath, Uriel the chief, and his brethren a hundred and twenty;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of the sons of Merari, Asaiah the chief, and his brethren two hundred and twenty;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of the sons of Gershom, Joel the chief, and his brethren a hundred and thirty;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of the sons of Elizaphan, Shemaiah the chief, and his brethren two hundred;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of the sons of Hebron, Eliel the chief, and his brethren eighty;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of the sons of Uzziel, Amminadab the chief, and his brethren a hundred and twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David called for Zadok and Abiathar the priests, and for the Levites, for Uriel, Asaiah, and Joel, Shemaiah, and Eliel, and Amminadab;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he said to them, Ye are the chief fathers of the Levites; hallow yourselves, ye and your brethren, that ye may bring up the ark of Jehovah theGod of Israel to [the place that] I have prepared for it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For because ye did [it] not at the first, Jehovah ourGod made a breach upon us, for that we sought him not after the due order.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So the priests and the Levites hallowed themselves to bring up the ark of Jehovah theGod of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of the Levites bore the ark ofGod upon their shoulders with the staves upon them, as Moses had commanded according to the word of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David spoke to the chief of the Levites to appoint their brethren, the singers, with instruments of music, lutes, and harps, and cymbals, that they should sound aloud, lifting up the voice with joy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Levites appointed Heman the son of Joel; and of his brethren, Asaph the son of Berechiah; and of the sons of Merari their brethren, Ethan the son of Kushaiah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and with them their brethren of the second [rank], Zechariah, Ben, and Jaaziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Unni, Eliab, and Benaiah, and Maaseiah, and Mattithiah, and Elipheleh, and Mikneiah, and Obed-Edom, and Jeiel, the doorkeepers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the singers, Heman, Asaph, and Ethan, to sound aloud with cymbals of brass;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Zechariah, and Aziel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Unni, and Eliab, and Maaseiah, and Benaiah, with lutes on Alamoth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Mattithiah, and Elipheleh, and Mikneiah, and Obed-Edom and Jeiel, and Azaziah, with harps on the Sheminith to lead [the singing].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Chenaniah, chief of the Levites for the music, gave instruction in music, for he was skilful.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Berechiah and Elkanah were doorkeepers for the ark.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Shebaniah, and Jehoshaphat, and Nethaneel, and Amasai, and Zechariah, and Benaiah, and Eliezer, the priests, blew with the trumpets before the ark ofGod; and Obed-Edom and Jehijah were doorkeepers for the ark.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David, and the elders of Israel, and the captains over thousands, went to bring up the ark of the covenant of Jehovah out of the house of Obed-Edom with joy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, whenGod helped the Levites that bore the ark of the covenant of Jehovah, that they sacrificed seven bullocks and seven rams.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David was clothed with a robe of byssus, and all the Levites that bore the ark, and the singers, and Chenaniah chief of the music of the singers; and David had upon him a linen ephod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of Jehovah with shouting, and with sound of the trumpet, and with clarions, and with cymbals, playing aloud with lutes and harps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as the ark of the covenant of Jehovah came to the city of David, that Michal the daughter of Saul looked through a window, and saw king David dancing and playing; and she despised him in her heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they brought in the ark ofGod, and set it in the midst of the tent that David had spread for it; and they presented burnt-offerings and peace-offerings beforeGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when David had ended offering up the burnt-offerings and the peace-offerings, he blessed the people in the name of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he dealt to every one of Israel, both man and woman, to every one a loaf of bread and a measure [of wine] and a raisin-cake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he appointed certain of the Levites to do the service before the ark of Jehovah, and to celebrate, and to thank and praise Jehovah theGod of Israel:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Asaph the chief, and second to him Zechariah, Jeiel, and Shemiramoth, and Jehiel, and Mattithiah, and Eliab, and Benaiah and Obed-Edom, and Jeiel, with instruments of lutes and with harps; and Asaph sounding with cymbals;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Benaiah and Jahaziel the priests with trumpets continually, before the ark of the covenant ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then on that day David delivered first [this psalm] to give thanks to Jehovah through Asaph and his brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give thanks unto Jehovah, call upon his name; Make known his acts among the peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing unto him, sing psalms unto him; Meditate upon all his wondrous works.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Glory in his holy name: Let the heart of them rejoice that seek Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Seek Jehovah and his strength, Seek his face continually;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember his wondrous works which he hath done; His miracles, and the judgments of his mouth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye seed of Israel his servant, Ye sons of Jacob, his chosen ones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He, Jehovah, is ourGod; His judgments are in all the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be ye ever mindful of his covenant, The word which he commanded to a thousand generations, —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which he made with Abraham, And of his oath unto Isaac;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he confirmed it unto Jacob for a statute, Unto Israel for an everlasting covenant,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Saying, Unto thee will I give the land of Canaan, The lot of your inheritance;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When ye were a few men in number, Of small account, and strangers in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went from nation to nation, And from one kingdom to another people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He suffered no man to oppress them, And reproved kings for their sakes,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Saying,] Touch not mine anointed ones, And do my prophets no harm.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sing unto Jehovah, all the earth; Publish his salvation from day to day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Declare his glory among the nations, His wondrous works among all peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Jehovah is great, and exceedingly to be praised; And he is terrible above allgods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For all thegods of the peoples are idols; But Jehovah made the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Majesty and splendour are before him; Strength and gladness in his place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give unto Jehovah, ye families of peoples, Give unto Jehovah glory and strength!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give unto Jehovah the glory of his name! Bring an oblation, and come before him: Worship Jehovah in holy splendour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Tremble before him, all the earth: The world also is established, it shall not be moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad; And let them say among the nations, Jehovah reigneth!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof; Let the field exult, and all that is therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shall the trees of the forest sing out at the presence of Jehovah, For he cometh to judge the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give thanks unto Jehovah, for he is good; For his loving-kindness [endureth] for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And say, Save us, OGod of our salvation, And gather us, and deliver us from the nations, To give thanks unto thy holy name, To triumph in thy praise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed be Jehovah theGod of Israel, from eternity and to eternity! And all the people said, Amen! and praised Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he left there, before the ark of the covenant of Jehovah, Asaph and his brethren, to do the service before the ark continually, as every day's duty required;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Obed-Edom, and their brethren, sixty-eight; Obed-Edom also, the son of Jeduthun, and Hosah as doorkeepers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zadok the priest, and his brethren the priests, before the tabernacle of Jehovah in the high place that was at Gibeon,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to offer up burnt-offerings to Jehovah on the altar of burnt-offering continually, morning and evening, and according to all that is written in the law of Jehovah, which he commanded Israel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and with them Heman and Jeduthun, and the rest that were chosen, who were expressed by name, to give thanks to Jehovah, because his loving-kindness [endureth] for ever;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and with them, [with] Heman and Jeduthun, trumpets and cymbals for those that should sound aloud; and the musical instruments ofGod. And the sons of Jeduthun were at the gate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the people departed every one to his house; and David returned to bless his household.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as David dwelt in his house, that David said to Nathan the prophet, Behold, I dwell in a house of cedars, and the ark of the covenant of Jehovah under curtains.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nathan said to David, Do all that is in thy heart; forGod is with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass that night that the word ofGod came to Nathan saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go and say to David my servant, Thus saith Jehovah: Thou shalt not build me a house to dwell in;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I have not dwelt in a house since the day that I brought up Israel to this day; but I have been from tent to tent, and from [one] tabernacle [to another].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In all my going about with all Israel, did I speak a word to any of the judges of Israel, whom I commanded to feed my people, saying, Why build ye me not a house of cedars?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, thus shalt thou say unto my servant David, Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: I took thee from the pasture-grounds, from following the sheep, to be prince over my people Israel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I have been with thee whithersoever thou wentest, and have cut off all thine enemies from before thee, and have made thee a name, like unto the name of the great men that are on the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will appoint a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, that they may dwell in a place of their own, and be disturbed no more; neither shall the sons of wickedness waste them any more, as formerly,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and since the days that I commanded judges to be over my people Israel. And I will subdue all thine enemies; and I tell thee that Jehovah will build thee a house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, when thy days are fulfilled that thou must go [to be] with thy fathers, that I will set up thy seed after thee, which shall be of thy sons; and I will establish his kingdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is he who shall build me a house, and I will establish his throne for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will be his father, and he shall be my son; and I will not take away my mercy from him, as I took it from him that was before thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will settle him in my house and in my kingdom for ever; and his throne shall be established for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so did Nathan speak to David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king David went in and sat before Jehovah, and said, Who am I, Jehovah Elohim, and what is my house, that thou hast brought me hitherto?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this hath been a small thing in thy sight, OGod; and thou hast spoken of thy servant's house for a great while to come, and hast regarded me according to the rank of a man of high degree, Jehovah Elohim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What can David [say] more to thee for the glory of thy servant? thou indeed knowest thy servant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, for thy servant's sake, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all this greatness, to make known all these great things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, there is none like thee, neither is there anyGod beside thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And who is like thy people Israel, the one nation in the earth thatGod went to redeem to be a people to himself, to make thee a name of greatness and terribleness, by driving out nations from before thy people, which thou hast redeemed out of Egypt?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thy people Israel hast thou made thine own people for ever; and thou, Jehovah, art become theirGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, Jehovah, let the word that thou hast spoken concerning thy servant and concerning his house be established for ever, and do as thou hast said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let it even be established, and let thy name be magnified for ever, saying, Jehovah of hosts, theGod of Israel, isGod to Israel; and let the house of David thy servant be established before thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou, myGod, hast revealed to thy servant that thou wilt build him a house; therefore hath thy servant found [in his heart] to pray before thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, Jehovah, thou art thatGod, and hast promised this goodness unto thy servant;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and now, let it please thee to bless the house of thy servant, that it may be before thee for ever; for thou, Jehovah, hast blessed [it], and it shall be blessed for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after this it came to pass that David smote the Philistines, and subdued them, and took Gath and its towns out of the hand of the Philistines.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he smote the Moabites; and the Moabites became David's servants, [and] brought gifts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David smote Hadarezer king of Zobah, at Hamath, as he went to establish his dominion by the river Euphrates.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David took from him a thousand chariots, and seven thousand horsemen, and twenty thousand footmen; and David houghed all the chariot [horses], but reserved of them [for] a hundred chariots.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadarezer king of Zobah, and David smote of the Syrians twenty-two thousand men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David put [garrisons] in Syria of Damascus; and the Syrians became servants to David, [and] brought gifts. And Jehovah preserved David whithersoever he went.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David took the shields of gold that were on the servants of Hadarezer, and brought them to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And from Tibhath, and from Chun, cities of Hadarezer, David took very much brass, of which Solomon made the brazen sea, and the pillars, and the vessels of brass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Tou king of Hamath heard that David had smitten all the forces of Hadarezer king of Zobah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he sent Hadoram his son to king David to inquire of his welfare, and to congratulate him, because he had fought against Hadarezer and smitten him; for Hadarezer was continually at war with Tou; [he sent] also all manner of vessels of gold and silver and bronze.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Them also king David dedicated to Jehovah, with the silver and the gold that he had brought from all the nations: from the Edomites, and from the Moabites, and from the children of Ammon, and from the Philistines, and from the Amalekites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abishai the son of Zeruiah smote of the Edomites in the valley of salt eighteen thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he put garrisons in Edom; and all they of Edom became servants to David. And Jehovah preserved David whithersoever he went.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David reigned over all Israel, and executed judgment and justice to all his people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joab the son of Zeruiah was over the host; and Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud was chronicler;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Zadok the son of Ahitub, and Abimelech the son of Abiathar, were the priests; and Shavsha was scribe;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites; and the sons of David were the first at the king's hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after this that Nahash king of the children of Ammon died, and his son reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said, I will shew kindness to Hanun the son of Nahash, because his father shewed kindness to me. And David sent messengers to comfort him concerning his father. And David's servants came into the land of the children of Ammon to Hanun, to comfort him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the princes of the children of Ammon said to Hanun, Is it, in thine eyes, to honour thy father that David has sent comforters to thee? Is it not to search and overthrow, and to spy out the land that his servants are come to thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hanun took David's servants, and had them shaved, and their raiment cut off in the midst, as far as the hip, and sent them away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [certain] went and told David concerning the men; and he sent to meet them, for the men were greatly ashamed. And the king said, Abide at Jericho until your beards be grown, and then return.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Ammon saw that they had made themselves odious to David; and Hanun and the children of Ammon sent a thousand talents of silver to hire them chariots and horsemen out of Mesopotamia, and from the Syrians of Maacah, and from Zobah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they hired thirty-two thousand chariots, and the king of Maacah and his people; and they came and encamped before Medeba. And the children of Ammon gathered together from their cities, and came to battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David heard [of it], and he sent Joab, and all the host, the mighty men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Ammon came out, and put the battle in array at the entrance of the city; and the kings that had come were by themselves on the field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joab saw that the front of the battle was against him before and behind; and he chose out of all the choice men of Israel, and put them in array against the Syrians;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the rest of the people he gave into the hand of Abishai his brother, and they set themselves in array against the children of Ammon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, If the Syrians be too strong for me, then thou shalt help me; and if the children of Ammon be too strong for thee, then I will help thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be strong, and let us shew ourselves valiant for our people, and for the cities of ourGod; and Jehovah will do what is good in his sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joab and the people that were with him drew near in front of the Syrians to the battle; and they fled before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the children of Ammon saw that the Syrians fled, they likewise fled before Abishai his brother, and entered into the city. And Joab came to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the Syrians saw that they were routed before Israel, they sent messengers, and drew forth the Syrians that were beyond the river; and Shophach the captain of the host of Hadarezer [went] before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told David; and he gathered all Israel, and passed over the Jordan, and came upon them, and set [the battle] in array against them. And David put the battle in array against the Syrians, and they fought with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Syrians fled from before Israel; and David slew of the Syrians seven thousand [in] chariots, and forty thousand footmen, and slew Shophach the captain of the host.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the servants of Hadarezer saw that they were routed before Israel, and they made peace with David, and became his servants. And the Syrians would no more help the children of Ammon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the time of the return of the year, at the time when kings go forth, that Joab led forth the power of the army, and laid waste the land of the children of Ammon, and came and besieged Rabbah. But David abode at Jerusalem. And Joab smote Rabbah, and overthrew it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David took the crown of their king from off his head, and found it to weigh a talent of gold, and there were precious stones in it; and it was [set] upon David's head: and he brought forth the spoil of the city in great abundance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he brought out the people that were in it, and cut them with the saw, and with harrows of iron, and with saws. And so David did to all the cities of the children of Ammon. And David and all the people returned to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after this, that there arose war at Gezer with the Philistines; then Sibbechai the Hushathite smote Sippai, one of the children of Rapha; and they were subdued.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was again a battle with the Philistines; and Elhanan the son of Jair smote Lahmi the brother of Goliath the Gittite; now the shaft of his spear was like a weaver's beam.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was again a battle, at Gath; and there was a man [there] of great stature, whose fingers [and toes] were four and twenty, six [on each hand], and six [on each foot]; and he also was born to Rapha.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he defied Israel; but Jonathan the son of Shimea David's brother smote him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These were born to Rapha in Gath; and they fell by the hand of David, and by the hand of his servants.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Satan stood up against Israel, and moved David to number Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Joab and to the princes of the people, Go, number Israel from Beer-sheba even to Dan; and bring the number of them to me, that I may know it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joab said, Jehovah add to his people, how many soever they be, a hundredfold: are they not all, my lord O king, my lord's servants? why does my lord require this thing? why should he become a trespass to Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the king's word prevailed against Joab; and Joab departed, and went through all Israel, and came [again] to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joab gave the sum of the number of the people to David. And all they of Israel were eleven hundred thousand men that drew sword; and of Judah, four hundred and seventy thousand men that drew sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Levi and Benjamin he did not count among them; for the king's word was abominable to Joab.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod was displeased on account of this thing, and he smote Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said toGod, I have sinned greatly, in that I have done this thing; and now, I beseech thee, put away the iniquity of thy servant; for I have done very foolishly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to Gad, David's seer, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go and speak to David saying, Thus saith Jehovah: I offer thee three [things]; choose one of them, that I may do it unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Gad came to David, and said to him, Thus saith Jehovah:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Choose thee, either three years of famine, or three months to be destroyed before thine adversaries while that the sword of thine enemies overtaketh thee, or three days the sword of Jehovah and the pestilence in the land, and the angel of Jehovah destroying through all the borders of Israel. And now consider what word I shall bring again to him that sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Gad, I am in a great strait: let me fall, I pray thee, into the hand of Jehovah, for his mercies are very great; but let me not fall into the hand of man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah sent a pestilence upon Israel; and there fell of Israel seventy thousand men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod sent an angel to Jerusalem to destroy it; and as he was destroying, Jehovah beheld, and he repented him of the evil, and said to the angel that destroyed, It is enough; withdraw now thine hand. And the angel of Jehovah stood by the threshing-floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David lifted up his eyes, and saw the angel of Jehovah stand between the earth and the heavens, and his sword drawn in his hand, stretched out over Jerusalem. And David and the elders, clothed in sackcloth, fell on their faces.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said toGod, Is it not I that commanded the people to be numbered? It is I that have sinned and done evil; but these sheep, what have they done? Let thy hand, I pray thee, Jehovah myGod, be on me and on my father's house; but not on thy people, that they should be smitten.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the angel of Jehovah commanded Gad to say to David, that David should go up and rear an altar to Jehovah in the threshing-floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David went up at the saying of Gad, which he had spoken in the name of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ornan turned back and saw the angel; and his four sons with him hid themselves. Now Ornan was threshing wheat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David came to Ornan, and Ornan looked and saw David, and went out of the threshing-floor, and bowed himself to David with [his] face to the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Ornan, Grant me the place of the threshing-floor, that I may build an altar in it to Jehovah: grant it to me for the full money, that the plague may be stayed from the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ornan said to David, Take it to thee, and let my lord the king do that which is good in his sight: see, I give the oxen for the burnt-offering, and the threshing-sledges for wood, and the wheat for the oblation; I give it all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king David said to Ornan, No; but I will in any case buy [them] for the full money; for I will not take that which is thine for Jehovah, to offer up a burnt-offering without cost.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David gave to Ornan for the place in shekels of gold the weight of six hundred [shekels].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David built there an altar to Jehovah, and offered up burnt-offerings and peace-offerings, and called upon Jehovah; and he answered him from the heavens by fire upon the altar of burnt-offering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah spoke to the angel; and he put up his sword again into its sheath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time when David saw that Jehovah had answered him in the threshing-floor of Ornan the Jebusite, then he sacrificed there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the tabernacle of Jehovah, which Moses had made in the wilderness, and the altar of burnt-offering, were at that time in the high place at Gibeon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But David could not go before it to inquire ofGod; for he was afraid because of the sword of the angel of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said, This is the house of Jehovah Elohim, and this is the altar of burnt-offering for Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David commanded to collect the strangers that were in the land of Israel; and he set masons to hew wrought stones to build the house ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David prepared iron in abundance for the nails for the doors of the gates, and for the joists, and brass in abundance without weight;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and cedar-trees innumerable; for the Zidonians and they of Tyre brought cedar-wood in abundance to David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For David said, Solomon my son is young and tender, and the house that is to be built for Jehovah must be exceeding great in fame and in beauty in all lands: I will therefore make preparation for it. And David prepared abundantly before his death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he called for Solomon his son, and charged him to build a house for Jehovah theGod of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Solomon, As for me, my son, I was minded to build a house unto the name of Jehovah myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the word of Jehovah came to me saying, Thou hast shed blood abundantly, and hast made great wars: thou shalt not build a house unto my name, for thou hast shed much blood upon the earth in my sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, a son shall be born to thee, who shall be a man of rest; and I will give him rest from all his enemies round about; for his name shall be Solomon, and in his days I will give peace and quietness unto Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall build a house unto my name; and he shall be my son, and I will be his father; and I will establish the throne of his kingdom over Israel for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now, my son, Jehovah be with thee, that thou mayest prosper, and build the house of Jehovah thyGod, as he has said of thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only, Jehovah give thee wisdom and understanding, and place thee over Israel, and to keep the law of Jehovah thyGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shalt thou prosper, if thou takest heed to perform the statutes and ordinances which Jehovah commanded Moses for Israel: be strong and courageous; fear not, neither be dismayed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, in my affliction I have prepared for the house of Jehovah a hundred thousand talents of gold, and a thousand thousand talents of silver; and of brass and iron without weight, for it is in abundance; and timber and stone have I prepared; and thou shalt add to it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there are workmen with thee in abundance, hewers and workers of stone and timber, and all manner of skilful men for every kind of work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the gold, the silver, and the brass, and the iron, there is no number. Arise and be doing and Jehovah be with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David commanded all the princes of Israel to help Solomon his son, [saying,]

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is not Jehovah yourGod with you? and has he [not] given you rest on every side? for he has given the inhabitants of the land into my hand; and the land is subdued before Jehovah, and before his people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now set your heart and your soul to seek Jehovah yourGod; and arise and build the sanctuary of Jehovah Elohim, to bring the ark of the covenant of Jehovah, and the vessels of the sanctuary ofGod into the house that is to be built unto the name of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David was old and full of days; and he made Solomon his son king over Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he gathered together all the princes of Israel, with the priests and the Levites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Levites were numbered from thirty years old and upward; and their number, by their polls, man by man, was thirty-eight thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of these, twenty-four thousand were to preside over the work of the house of Jehovah; and six thousand were officers and judges;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and four thousand were doorkeepers; and four thousand praised Jehovah with the instruments which I made, [said David,] to praise [therewith].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David divided them into courses according to the sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the Gershonites: Laadan and Shimei.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Laadan: the head was Jehiel, and Zetham, and Joel, three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Shimei: Shelomith, and Haziel, and Haran, three. These were the chief fathers of Laadan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Shimei: Jahath, Ziza, and Jeush, and Beriah. These were the four sons of Shimei.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jahath was the head, and Ziza the second; and Jeush and Beriah had not many sons: as father's house, therefore, they were reckoned as one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Jizhar, Hebron, and Uzziel, four.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Amram: Aaron and Moses. And Aaron was separated, that he should be hallowed as most holy, he and his sons for ever, to offer before Jehovah, to do service to him, and to bless in his name for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And as to Moses the man ofGod, his sons were named of the tribe of Levi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Moses: Gershom and Eliezer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Gershom: Shebuel the head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Eliezer: Rehabiah the head; and Eliezer had no other sons, but the sons of Rehabiah were very many.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — The sons of Jizhar: Shelomith the head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Hebron: Jeriah the head, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Uzziel: Micah the head, and Jishijah the second.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The sons of Mahli: Eleazar and Kish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Eleazar died, and had no sons, but daughters; and their brethren the sons of Kish took them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Mushi: Mahli, and Eder, and Jeremoth, three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These were the sons of Levi according to their fathers' houses, the chief fathers, as they were reckoned, by number of names by their polls, who did the work of the service of the house of Jehovah, from twenty years old and upward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For David said, Jehovah theGod of Israel has given rest to his people, and he will dwell in Jerusalem for ever;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the Levites also have no more to carry the tabernacle, nor any of its vessels for its service.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For by the last words of David was this [done], — the numbering of the sons of Levi from twenty years old and upward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For their place was by the side of the sons of Aaron for the service of the house of Jehovah, over the courts, and over the chambers, and over the purifying of all holy things, and [for] the work of the service of the house ofGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and for the loaves to be set in rows, and for the fine flour for the oblation, and for the unleavened cakes, and for [what is baked in] the pan, and for that which is saturated [with oil], and for all measure of capacity and size;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to stand every morning to thank and praise Jehovah, and likewise at even;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and for all burnt-offerings offered up to Jehovah on the sabbaths, on the new moons, and on the set feasts, by number, according to the ordinance concerning them, continually, before Jehovah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they kept the charge of the tent of meeting, and the charge of the sanctuary, and the charge of the sons of Aaron their brethren, in the service of the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the divisions of the sons of Aaron: the sons of Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nadab and Abihu died before their father, and had no children; and Eleazar and Ithamar exercised the priesthood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David distributed them, both Zadok of the sons of Eleazar, and Ahimelech of the sons of Ithamar, according to their office in their service.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were more head-men found of the sons of Eleazar than of the sons of Ithamar, and [thus] were they divided: of the sons of Eleazar there were sixteen heads of fathers' houses, and eight of the sons of Ithamar according to their fathers' houses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were divided by lot, one with another; for the princes of the sanctuary and the princes ofGod were of the sons of Eleazar and of the sons of Ithamar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Shemaiah the son of Nethaneel the scribe, [one] of the Levites, inscribed them before the king, and the princes, and Zadok the priest, and Ahimelech the son of Abiathar, and the chief fathers of the priests and Levites: one father's house was drawn for Eleazar, and one drawn for Ithamar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the first lot came forth for Jehoiarib, the second for Jedaiah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the third for Harim, the fourth for Seorim,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the fifth for Malchijah, the sixth for Mijamin,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the seventh for Hakkoz, the eighth for Abijah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the ninth for Jeshuah, the tenth for Shecaniah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the eleventh for Eliashib, the twelfth for Jakim,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the thirteenth for Huppah, the fourteenth for Jeshebeab,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the fifteenth for Bilgah, the sixteenth for Immer,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the seventeenth for Hezir, the eighteenth for Happizez,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the nineteenth for Pethahiah, the twentieth for Ezekiel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the twenty-first for Jachin, the twenty-second for Gamul,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the twenty-third for Delaiah, the twenty-fourth for Maaziah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is their ordering in their service to come into the house of Jehovah, according to their ordinance, through Aaron their father, as Jehovah theGod of Israel had commanded him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as for the rest of the sons of Levi: of the sons of Amram, Shubael; of the sons of Shubael, Jehdeiah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of Rehabiah, of the sons of Rehabiah, the head was Jishijah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the Jizharites, Shelomoth; of the sons of Shelomoth, Jahath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the sons [of Hebron]: Jerijah [the head], Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, Jekameam the fourth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Uzziel, Micah; of the sons of Micah, Shamir;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the brother of Micah was Jishijah; of the sons of Jishijah, Zechariah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi; the sons of Jaaziah, his son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Merari by Jaaziah his son: Shoham, and Zaccur, and Ibri.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of Mahli: Eleazar, who had no sons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of Kish, the sons of Kish: Jerahmeel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sons of Mushi: Mahli, and Eder, and Jerimoth. These were the sons of the Levites according to their fathers' houses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These likewise cast lots just as their brethren the sons of Aaron before David the king, and Zadok, and Ahimelech, and the chief fathers of the priests and Levites, — the chief fathers just as the youngest of their brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David and the captains of the host separated for the service those of the sons of Asaph and of Heman and of Jeduthun who were to prophesy with harps and lutes and cymbals; and the number of the men employed according to their service was:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of the sons of Asaph: Zaccur, and Joseph, and Nethaniah, and Asharelah, the sons of Asaph under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied at the direction of the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of Jeduthun, the sons of Jeduthun: Gedaliah, and Zeri, and Isaiah, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah, [and Shimei] six, under the direction of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with the harp, to give thanks and to praise Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of Heman, the sons of Heman: Bukkijah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, and Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, and Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, Mahazioth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: all these were sons of Heman the king's seer in the words ofGod, to exalt his power; andGod had given to Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All these were under the direction of their fathers Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman, for song in the house of Jehovah, with cymbals, lutes and harps, for the service of the house ofGod, under the direction of the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the number of them, with their brethren that were instructed in the songs of Jehovah, all of them skilful, was two hundred and eighty-eight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they cast lots with one another over the charges, the small as well as the great, the teacher with the scholar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the first lot came forth for Asaph to Joseph; to Gedaliah the second: he and his brethren and his sons were twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The third to Zaccur; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fourth to Jizri; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fifth to Nethaniah; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sixth to Bukkijah; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The seventh to Jesharelah; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The eighth to Isaiah; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The ninth to Mattaniah; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The tenth to Shimei; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The eleventh to Azareel; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The twelfth to Hashabiah; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The thirteenth to Shubael; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fourteenth to Mattithiah; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fifteenth to Jeremoth; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sixteenth to Hananiah; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The seventeenth to Joshbekashah; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The eighteenth to Hanani; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The nineteenth to Mallothi; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The twentieth to Elijathah; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The twenty-first to Hothir; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The twenty-second to Giddalti; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The twenty-third to Mahazioth; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The twenty-fourth to Romamti-ezer; his sons and his brethren, twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The divisions of the doorkeepers. Of the Korahites: Meshelemiah the son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Meshelemiah had sons: Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Elam the fifth, Johanan the sixth, Elioenai the seventh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And the sons of Obed-Edom: Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, and Sacar the fourth, and Nethaneel the fifth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peulthai the eighth: forGod had blessed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And to Shemaiah his son were sons born, who were rulers in their father's house; for they were mighty men of valour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Shemaiah: Othni, and Rephael, and Obed, [and] Elzabad, whose brethren were valiant men, Elihu and Semachiah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All these were of the sons of Obed-Edom: they and their sons and their brethren, able men in strength for the service, were sixty-two of Obed-Edom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And Meshelemiah had sons and brethren, men of valour, eighteen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And Hosah, of the sons of Merari, had sons: Shimri the head, for though he was not the firstborn, yet his father made him the head;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hilkijah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zechariah the fourth: all the sons and brethren of Hosah were thirteen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Among these were the divisions of the doorkeepers, among the head-men, as to the charges together with their brethren, for performing the service in the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they cast lots, the small as well as the great, according to their fathers' houses, for every gate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the lot eastward fell to Shelemiah; and they cast lots for Zechariah his son, a wise counsellor, and his lot came out northward;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to Obed-Edom southward; and to his sons the storehouse.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To Shuppim and Hosah westward, with the gate Shallecheth, by the causeway of the ascent, watch against watch.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Eastward were six Levites, northward four a day, southward four a day, and in the storehouse two [and] two.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At the portico westward, four at the causeway, two at the portico.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These are the divisions of the doorkeepers among the sons of the Korahites and among the sons of Merari.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Levites: Ahijah was over the treasures of the house ofGod, and over the treasures of the dedicated things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Laadan, the sons of the Gershonites of Laadan, chief fathers of Laadan the Gershonite: Jehieli;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the sons of Jehieli: Zetham, and Joel his brother, over the treasures of the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As to the Amramites, the Jizharites, the Hebronites, the Uzzielites....

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Shebuel the son of Gershom, the son of Moses, was overseer of the treasures.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his brethren, of Eliezer: Rehabiah his son, and Isaiah his son, and Joram his son, and Zichri his son, and Shelomith his son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This Shelomith and his brethren were over all the treasures of the dedicated things, which king David, and the chief fathers, the captains over thousands and hundreds, and the captains of the host, had dedicated

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (from the wars and out of the spoils had they dedicated [them], to maintain the house of Jehovah),

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all that Samuel the seer, and Saul the son of Kish, and Abner the son of Ner, and Joab the son of Zeruiah had dedicated: all that was dedicated was under the hand of Shelomith and of his brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the Jizharites, Chenaniah and his sons were over Israel, for the outward business for officers and judges.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his brethren, men of valour, a thousand and seven hundred, for the administration of Israel on this side Jordan westward, for all the business of Jehovah, and for the service of the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the Hebronites was Jerijah the head; (as to the Hebronites, according to their families according to the fathers: in the fortieth year of the reign of David they were sought for, and there were found among them mighty men of valour at Jaazer in Gilead;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his brethren, men of valour, two thousand seven hundred chief fathers, whom king David made rulers over the Reubenites and the Gadites and the half tribe of the Manassites, for every matter pertaining toGod, and the affairs of the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [these] are the children of Israel after their number, the chief fathers and captains of thousands and hundreds, and their officers that served the king in every matter of the divisions, which came in and went out month by month throughout the months of the year; in every division were twenty-four thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Over the first division for the first month was Jashobeam the son of Zabdiel; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He was of the children of Pherez, the head of all the captains of the hosts for the first month.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And over the division of the second month was Dodai the Ahohite; and in his division was Mikloth ruler; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The third captain of the host for the third month was Benaiah (the son of Jehoiada, a principal officer): [he was] head; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This Benaiah was a mighty man among the thirty, and above the thirty; and in his division was Ammizabad his son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fourth for the fourth month was Asahel the brother of Joab, and Zebadiah his son after him; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The fifth for the fifth month was the captain Shamhuth the Jizrahite; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sixth for the sixth month was Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The seventh for the seventh month was Helez the Pelonite, of the children of Ephraim; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The eighth for the eighth month was Sibbechai the Hushathite, of the Zarhites; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The ninth for the ninth month was Abiezer the Anathothite, of the Benjaminites; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The tenth for the tenth month was Maharai the Netophathite, of the Zarhites; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The eleventh for the eleventh month was Benaiah the Pirathonite, of the children of Ephraim; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The twelfth for the twelfth month was Heldai the Netophathite, of Othniel; and in his division were twenty-four thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And over the tribes of Israel were: for the Reubenites Eliezer the son of Zichri was the prince; for the Simeonites, Shephatiah the son of Maachah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the Levites, Hashabiah the son of Kemuel; for Aaron, Zadok;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Judah, Elihu of the brethren of David; for Issachar, Omri the son of Michael;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Zebulun, Jishmaiah the son of Obadiah; for Naphtali, Jerimoth the son of Azriel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the children of Ephraim, Hosea the son of Azaziah; for the half tribe of Manasseh, Joel the son of Pedaiah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the half [tribe] of Manasseh in Gilead, Jiddo the son of Zechariah; for Benjamin, Jaasiel the son of Abner;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Dan, Azareel the son of Jeroham. These were the princes of the tribes of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David took not their number from twenty years old and under; for Jehovah had said he would increase Israel as the stars of heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Joab the son of Zeruiah began to number, but he did not finish; and there fell wrath for it upon Israel; and the number was not put in the account of the chronicles of king David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And over the king's treasures was Azmaveth the son of Adiel; and over the storehouses in the country, in the cities, and in the villages, and in the towers was Jonathan the son of Uzziah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And over them that worked in the field for tillage of the ground was Ezri the son of Chelub.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And over the vineyards was Shimei the Ramathite; and over what was in the vineyards of stores of wine was Zabdi the Shiphmite:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and over the olive-trees and the sycamore-trees that were in the lowland was Baal-hanan the Gederite; and over the cellars of oil was Joash.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And over the herds that fed in Sharon was Shitrai the Sharonite; and over the herds in the valleys was Shaphat the son of Adlai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And over the camels was Obil the Ishmaelite; and over the asses was Jehdiah the Meronothite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And over the flocks was Jaziz the Hagarite. All these were comptrollers of the substance which was king David's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan, David's uncle, was counsellor, a wise man, and a scribe; and Jehiel the son of Hachmoni was with the king's sons;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Ahithophel was the king's counsellor; and Hushai the Archite was the king's friend;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and after Ahithophel was Jehoiada the son of Benaiah, and Abiathar; and Joab was captain of the king's army.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David assembled all the princes of Israel, the princes of the tribes, and the princes of the divisions that ministered to the king, and the captains over thousands, and the captains over hundreds, and the comptrollers of all the substance and possessions of the king and of his sons, with the chamberlains, and the mighty men, and all the men of valour, unto Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king David stood up upon his feet, and said, Hear me, my brethren and my people! I had in my heart to build a house of rest for the ark of the covenant of Jehovah and for the footstool of ourGod, and I have prepared to build.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ButGod said to me, Thou shalt not build a house unto my name, for thou art a man of war, and hast shed blood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah theGod of Israel chose me out of all the house of my father to be king over Israel for ever; for he has chosen Judah to be the prince; and of the house of Judah, the house of my father; and among the sons of my father he took pleasure in me to make me king over all Israel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and of all my sons, (for Jehovah has given me many sons,) he has chosen Solomon my son to sit upon the throne of the kingdom of Jehovah over Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to me, Solomon thy son, he shall build my house and my courts; for I have chosen him to be my son, and I will be his father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will establish his kingdom for ever, if he be firm to do my commandments and mine ordinances, as at this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now in the sight of all Israel, the congregation of Jehovah, and in the audience of ourGod, — keep and seek for all the commandments of Jehovah yourGod; that ye may possess the good land, and leave it as an inheritance to your children after you for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, Solomon my son, know theGod of thy father, and serve him with a perfect heart and with a willing mind; for Jehovah searches all hearts, and discerns all the imaginations of the thoughts. If thou seek him, he will be found of thee; but if thou forsake him, he will cut thee off for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Consider now, that Jehovah has chosen thee to build a house for the sanctuary: be strong, and do [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David gave to Solomon his son the pattern of the porch, and of its houses, and of its treasuries, and of its upper chambers, and of its inner chambers, and of the house of the mercy-seat;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the pattern of all that he had by the Spirit, of the courts of the house of Jehovah, and of all the chambers round about, for the treasuries of the house ofGod, and for the treasuries of the dedicated things;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and for the courses of the priests and the Levites, and for all the work of the service of the house of Jehovah, and for all the instruments of service in the house of Jehovah:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: gold by weight for [things] of gold, for all utensils of each kind of service; for all utensils of silver, by weight, for all utensils of each kind of service;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the weight of the golden candlesticks, and of their golden lamps, by weight for every candlestick, and for its lamps; and for the silver candlesticks, by weight, for the candlestick and for its lamps, according to the use of every candlestick;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and gold by weight for the tables of the [loaves] to be set in rows, for every table; and silver for the tables of silver;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and pure gold for the forks, and the bowls, and the goblets; and for the golden basons by weight for every bason; and for the silver basons by weight for every bason;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and for the altar of incense, refined gold by weight; and the pattern of the chariot of the cherubim of gold, which spread out [their wings] and cover the ark of the covenant of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All this [said David,] in writing, by Jehovah's hand upon me, instructing as to all the works of the pattern.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to Solomon his son, Be strong and courageous, and do it; fear not nor be dismayed: for Jehovah Elohim, myGod, will be with thee; he will not leave thee, neither forsake thee, until all the work for the service of the house of Jehovah is finished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, the courses of the priests and the Levites are for all the service of the house ofGod; and thou hast with thee for all manner of workmanship every willing man, skilful for every sort of service; and the princes and all the people are wholly at thy commandment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king David said to all the congregation, Solomon my son, the one whomGod has chosen, is young and tender, and the work is great; for this palace is not to be for man, but for Jehovah Elohim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have prepared according to all my power for the house of myGod gold for [things of] gold, and silver for [things of] silver, and brass for [things of] brass, iron for [things of] iron, and wood for [things of] wood; onyx stones, and [stones] to be set, glistering stones, and of divers colours, and all manner of precious stones, and white marble in abundance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And moreover, in my affection for the house of myGod I have given of my own property of gold and silver, for the house of myGod, over and above all that I have prepared for the house of the sanctuary:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: three thousand talents of gold, of the gold of Ophir, and seven thousand talents of refined silver, to overlay the walls of the houses;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: gold for [things of] gold, and silver for [things of] silver, and for all manner of work by the hands of artificers. And who is willing to offer to Jehovah this day?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chief fathers and princes of the tribes of Israel, and the captains of thousands and of hundreds, and the comptrollers of the king's business, offered willingly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they gave for the service of the house ofGod five thousand talents and ten thousand darics of gold, and ten thousand talents of silver, and eighteen thousand talents of brass, and one hundred thousand talents of iron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they with whom stones were found gave [them] to the treasure of the house of Jehovah, into the hand of Jehiel the Gershonite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people rejoiced because they offered willingly, for with perfect heart they offered willingly to Jehovah; and David the king also rejoiced with great joy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David blessed Jehovah in the sight of all the congregation; and David said, Blessed be thou, Jehovah, theGod of our father Israel, for ever and ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thine, Jehovah, is the greatness, and the power, and the glory, and the splendour, and the majesty; for all that is in the heavens and on the earth is thine: thine, Jehovah, is the kingdom, and thou art exalted as Head above all;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and riches and glory are of thee, and thou rulest over everything; and in thy hand is power and might; and in thy hand it is to make all great and strong.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, ourGod, we thank thee, and praise thy glorious name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But who am I, and what is my people, that we should be able to offer willingly after this manner? for all is of thee, and of that which is from thy hand have we given thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we are strangers before thee, and sojourners, as all our fathers: our days on the earth are as a shadow, and there is no hope [of life].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah ourGod, all this store that we have prepared to build thee a house to thy holy name, is of thy hand, and is all thine own.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I know, myGod, that thou triest the heart, and hast pleasure in uprightness. In the uprightness of my heart have I willingly offered all these things; and now have I seen with joy thy people, which are present here, offer willingly to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah,God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Israel, our fathers, keep this for ever in the imagination of the thoughts of the heart of thy people, and direct their hearts to thee!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And give to Solomon my son a perfect heart, to keep thy commandments, thy testimonies, and thy statutes, and to do all, and to build the palace, for which I have made provision.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David said to all the congregation, Bless now Jehovah yourGod. And all the congregation blessed Jehovah theGod of their fathers, and bowed down their heads, and did homage to Jehovah and the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they sacrificed sacrifices to Jehovah, and offered up burnt-offerings to Jehovah, on the morrow after that day: a thousand bullocks, a thousand rams, a thousand lambs, with their drink-offerings, and sacrifices in abundance for all Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they ate and drank before Jehovah on that day with great joy. And they made Solomon the son of David king the second time, and anointed him to Jehovah to be prince, and Zadok to be priest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon sat on the throne of Jehovah as king instead of David his father, and prospered; and all Israel obeyed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the princes, and the mighty men, and all the sons likewise of king David, submitted themselves to Solomon the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah magnified Solomon exceedingly in the sight of all Israel, and bestowed upon him royal majesty such as had not been on any king before him in Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now David the son of Jesse reigned over all Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the time that he reigned over Israel was forty years: he reigned seven years in Hebron, and he reigned thirty-three years in Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he died in a good old age, full of days, riches, and honour; and Solomon his son reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the acts of David the king, first and last, behold, they are written in the book of Samuel the seer, and in the book of Nathan the prophet, and in the book of Gad the seer;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: with all his reign and his might, and the times that passed over him, and over Israel, and over all the kingdoms of the countries.
